task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the medical record of patient Jayson Fadel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR interface displays 'Jayson Fadel' (DOB: 1992-06-30) in the patient header, dashboard, or search results.

task_completion.description: Agent locates the active prescription for amLODIPine / Hydrochlorothiazide / Olmesartan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Medications' or 'Prescriptions' list is visible, showing 'amLODIPine 5 MG / Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 MG / Olmesartan medoxomil 20 MG' (or a recognizable substring of this combination).

task_completion.description: Agent opens the edit or discontinue interface for the target medication.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the end date and documents the clinical reason for discontinuation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'End Date' field is populated (e.g., with today's date) and a text field (Reason/Note/Comments) contains keywords like 'peripheral edema', 'ankle swelling', or 'side effect'.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the changes and the medication is visually confirmed as discontinued or inactive.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The medication list is displayed showing the target medication in an 'Inactive' section, with a visible end date, or crossed out/marked as discontinued.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the OpenEMR web interface rather than backend scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser displaying the OpenEMR application, not a terminal or code editor performing database updates.

integrity.description: Modifications are performed strictly on Jayson Fadel's chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: The patient name 'Jayson Fadel' remains visible in the header or context area while the medication is being modified.

integrity.description: Agent does not use external text editors to fabricate a success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Jayson Fadel (DOB: 1992-06-30). Medication to stop: amLODIPine 5 MG / Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 MG / Olmesartan medoxomil 20 MG. Valid reasons for discontinuation include 'peripheral edema', 'ankle swelling', or 'side effect'.
